subject: Ecology
title: Unusual Pollinators
description: A micro-lesson that makes Unusual Pollinators usable.

hook.text: Flies, beetles, lizards, and even rodents pollinate flowers—not just the famous bees.

buildup.text: Flies pollinate many tropical flowers, especially those that mimic the smell of rotting meat.

discovery.text: Beetles were among the first pollinators, visiting flowers 200 million years before bees evolved.

twist.text: In South Africa, gerbils pollinate ground-level proteas by sticking their noses into blooms.

climax.text: Pollination networks are more diverse than most people realize—bees are just the celebrities.

punchline.text: Almost anything that visits a flower can pollinate it.

quiz.question: What were among the first animal pollinators?
